ogTitle: Johnsons Hill vs Johnstown: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Johnsons Hill and Johnstown.
heading: Johnsons Hill vs Johnstown — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Johnsons Hill, Queensland and Johnstown, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Johnsons Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Johnstown (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Johnsons Hill or Johnstown safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Johnsons Hill has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Johnstown scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Johnsons Hill scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Johnstown scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Johnsons Hill or Johnstown?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Johnsons Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
